description: This is a sample input for the XGBoost + Isolation Forest spending trend detection model.

notes.features_overview: 20-dimensional feature vector computed per user-category-month by compute_features.py.
notes.rolling_means: Exponentially weighted moving averages over 1, 3, and 6 month windows. Captures short and long-term spending trends.
notes.rolling_stds: Standard deviation over 3 and 6 month windows. Measures spending volatility.
notes.deviation_ratio: current_spend / rolling_mean_3m. Values > 1.0 indicate above-average spending.
notes.share_of_wallet: Fraction of total monthly spend allocated to this category.
notes.spending_velocity: Rate of change in spending: rolling_mean_1m / rolling_mean_3m.
notes.budget_utilization: current_spend / user-defined budget for this category (1.0 = at budget, >1.0 = over budget).
notes.elevated_cat_count: Number of categories where the user is currently spending above their 3-month average.
